title: Thliphthisa pusilla
intro: Thliphthia pusilla, also known as Thliphthia pusilla or Plectranthus pungu, is a flowering plant species in the family Lamiaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory agent, relieving symptoms of fever and cough, and also exhibiting antimicrobial properties that aid in wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Thliphthia pusilla include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Thliphthia pusilla is commonly used in traditional medicine, and its extracts are prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Thliphthisa pusilla belongs to the family of parasitic plants known as Loranthaceae. It is classified as a species of flowering plant. Thliphthisa pusilla is native to Australia, and as a part of its classification, it is recognized as an epiphyte. The species is classified as an obligate hemiparasite. Thliphthisa pusilla further belongs to the order Santalales.
